Purpose: quick, readable handoff for the reviewer (internal prep) — capture open control gaps, proposed short-term mitigations (POA), owners, and what to bring to next audit sync.

Context: prepping for the week-of-03-09 security & compliance review with external assessor. Focus areas: user access reviews (IA), change management (CM), vendor onboarding and attestations (VR), and incident response tabletop evidence (IR).

High-level status (notes to self):
- IA (Identity & Access): recurring reviews set up but scoping mismatch for cloud-admin role. Evidence: last review 2026-01-15 (export attached in confluence). Gap: no proof of recertification for two cloud-admins (contractor + new hire).
 - Quick POA: run ad-hoc recertify report, get sign-off from team leads. Owner: Jordan. ETA: 2026-03-01 (ask for confirmation during 1:1).

text:
Doc purpose
- Establish baseline marketplace onboarding metrics prior to the listing refresh + onboarding hardening.
- Capture where we lose users (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → API key → first successful request).
- Identify instrumentation gaps blocking reliable reporting.

Data sources & notes (read before using numbers)
- AWS Marketplace: partner portal lead exports + internal entitlement sync logs (partial) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=aws” deals.
- GCP Marketplace: Cloud Commerce Partner lead feed (manual export) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=gcp” + console onboarding logs (partial).
- Console steps are undercounted: we cannot currently join user sessions reliably across “subscribe” (external) and “provisioning” (our console) for all flows.
- Time window for baseline: 2025-12-15 to 2026-02-07 (8 weeks). For QoQ comparisons, see note at bottom.

text:
Purpose / how to use this backlog
- Working list of ideas for the 4-week Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention).
- Prioritize by ICE. This is intentionally messy; we’ll prune weekly.
- ICE definition (rough): Impact (1-10), Confidence (1-10), Effort (1-10). ICE Score = (Impact * Confidence) / Effort.
- Notes column includes open questions + gotchas + “we should ask ___”.

Guardrails / non-goals (sticky notes)
- Do NOT target active enterprise opps w/ retargeting. Jordan/Stephanie to provide exclusion list; Tyler to implement in audiences.
- CAN-SPAM/GDPR: unsubscribe + preferences must be respected everywhere (email sends + in-console surfaces). See ENG-10518 + PR-28505.
- Measurement integrity: event taxonomy + dedupe is mandatory before we judge lifts. ENG-10492 + PR-28431 + PR-28521.
- Don’t ship “big” UX changes; keep to nudges/checklist + li...

text:
Owner: Ben Carter
Ops/Attribution: Tyler Benson
Sales exclusions + handoff rules: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen
Week: 3 (retargeting launch + first performance read)
Date range: 2026-01-28 → 2026-02-03 (7 days)

TL;DR
- Retargeting is live across LinkedIn + Google Display with conservative spend caps and strict sales opp exclusions.
- Best early signal: “Signup but no activation (no first request)” cohort shows strongest click intent and the most assisted activation lift.
- Weakest: “General site visitors” is cheap traffic but low downstream quality; keep it as small top-of-funnel retargeting only.
- Creative: “Hello World in 60s” / “First request snippet” variants outperform generic value-prop (latency/cost/reliability) on CTR and time-on-page.
- Attribution caveat: still early, and activation lag + cross-device means we should treat results as directional until next week.

Goal / ...

Purpose
- Standard weekly template for lifecycle growth experiments (email onboarding, in-console nudges, retargeting).
- Designed to force consistent definitions, comparable reporting, and clear keep/iterate/kill decisions.

How to use
- Fill this out for every experiment weekly (even if results are “too early”).
- If metrics changed due to instrumentation fixes (e.g., dedupe), explicitly call it out in “Data quality & caveats.”
- Keep the top summary short enough to paste into Slack + exec email.

We’re optimizing for self-serve activation + early retention. These are retargeting ads (not prospecting) aimed at developers who already touched Redwood (visited site / started signup / signed up but stalled).

Secondary goals:

- Re-activate users who did first request but didn’t return (D7 activity)

1) TL;DR (what we learned)
- Variant B (subject line + opening value prop) won on opens and click-to-console, and is trending positive on early activation l...
text:
Doc: Week 1 Readout (Draft) — Onboarding Email v2 A/B
Owner: Ben Carter
Date: 2026-01-21
Sprint: Lifecycle growth experiments (Hosted API activation/retention)

1) TL;DR (what we learned)
- Variant B (subject line + opening value prop) won on opens and click-to-console, and is trending positive on early activation leading indicators.
- Biggest drop-off continues to be between “signed up” → “API key created” for net-new users (esp. Gmail domains, likely hobbyists) and between “API key created” → “first request” for small teams that hit SDK/config friction.
- No deliverability red flags this week, but we’re still in conservative ramp mode; a small uptick in soft bounces came from a subset of EU addresses (needs monitoring).

2) Hypothesis + experiment summary
Hypothesis
- Clearer, performance-oriented messaging (latency/reliability) + a single “make your first request” CTA will increase...

Last updated: 2026-01-18 (Ben)

Purpose
- Working copy doc for Onboarding Email v2 (Hosted API).
- Includes: subject line sets, body variants, CTA tests, and rationale.
- Includes reviewer comments + open questions.

Primary goal
- Lift activation: % of new signups completing (1) first successful request and (2) first 100k tokens within 7 days.

Secondary goals
- Reduce time-to-first-token (TTFT) by pushing toward “first request” snippet.
- Improve D7 return (nudge to usage dashboard + streaming).

Guiding messaging (from guidelines)
- Developer-first, production-grade inference.
- Emphasize: predictable latency, cost controls, reliability. Avoid overpromising.
- Keep “fast to first request” as the hero, with credibility cues (observability, fallbacks, dedicated/private path later).

text:
Working doc — v1 copy for benchmark report lifecycle emails.

Audience + goal
- PLG / self-serve: drive traffic to interactive benchmark page → signup / generate API key → first call within 24h.
- Enterprise leads (Dedicated/Private): drive landing page engagement → request a walkthrough / sizing call → book meeting.

Notes (claims / legal)
- Avoid absolute superlatives (e.g., “fastest”, “best”, “#1”).
- Use “in our tests”, “measured under the methodology described here”, “results vary by workload, region, and configuration”.
- If mentioning competitor baselines: only if approved in final report; otherwise keep it Redwood-only and methodology-forward.
- Always include a short disclaimer line + link to methodology summary on landing page.

Primary assets to link
- Interactive benchmark page (primary): https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar
- PDF report (secondary): ...

text:
Owner: Ben
Date: 2025-06-17 (compiled from notes across Weeks 1–8)
Audience: Marketing/Growth + PMM + Sales + SE + Security/Compliance + CS

Context / what this is
- This is an informal retro on the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot targeting regulated workloads (initially FinServ + Healthcare).
- Focus was Dedicated + Private (VPC/on‑prem). We explicitly tried to avoid pulling in “net-new product,” except minor docs/enablement.
- Goal of this doc: capture what worked, what didn’t, where we got stuck (esp. security review), and what we should change before we scale.

Quick outcome snapshot (directionally, not a KPI report)
- FinServ produced the highest-quality conversations fastest; Healthcare had the most variability (PHI sensitivity + on‑prem/networking blockers).
- 2 meaningful pilots/POC tracks ran in parallel (NorthBridge, Helix). Both required heavy SE + Security involvement and “MAP...

text:
Context / what this sheet is
- Goal: pick initial “sales-worthy” cut lines for the NA pilot (minimize spam, catch real scale/compliance intent).
- Cohort: self-serve orgs with >$200 total spend in last 30d OR >2M tokens total in last 30d.
- Time window: 2025-03-25 to 2025-04-23 (30d), with 7d deltas for velocity.
- Exclusions: internal/test orgs, sandbox workspaces, known agency/reseller accounts (tracked in allow/deny list; not fully clean yet).
- Notes: numbers below are directional (telemetry lag + billing reconciliation can shift spend by ~1–3%).

Data sources / definitions
- tokens_30d: total generated tokens (text+chat) in last 30d.
- tokens_day_avg_30d: tokens_30d / 30.
- spend_30d_usd: usage-based billed amount (net of free credits, pre-tax).
- spend_velocity_7d_wow: (spend last 7d) / (spend prior 7d). 1.0 = flat.
- req_429_rate_7d: 429 responses / total requests over last 7d ...

text:
Last updated: 2025-05-27 (Ben)

Purpose
- Brainstorm + converge on a canonical list of “sales-worthy” PLG signals for Hosted → Dedicated / Private upgrades.
- Propose initial thresholds for NA pilot; expect tuning after week 1–2.
- Capture open questions + early stakeholder interview notes.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- Final scoring model (that will live in ADR / PRD)
- Final HubSpot workflows, task templates

Glossary / framing
- Signal = an aggregated, privacy-aware indicator of (a) scale, (b) pain (reliability/cost), or (c) intent (upgrade/compliance).
- We should separate:
 1) Threshold signals (“they’re big / growing”) and
 2) Intent signals (“they’re looking for Dedicated/Private/security/pricing”).
- Ideal signal qualities:
 - actionability (AE/SE can do something within 24–48 hours)
 - specificity (not just “they used the product”)
 - low spam: avoid triggering on single-page c...

Context / goal
- Audience: Financial services accounts evaluating GenAI/LLM workloads where security/compliance + predictable performance are gating factors.
- Primary CTA: 20-min “security + architecture” intro (loop in Security/SE as needed). Secondary CTA: 2-page security FAQ + evidence pack index request.

Compliance / claims guardrails (use approved language)
- DO say: “SOC 2 Type II available under NDA”, “encryption in transit and at rest”, “KMS integrations supported in Private deployments”, “audit logs and role-based access controls”, “data retention controls by deployment mode / contract”.
- DO NOT say: “certified for <reg>” unless explicitly verified; avoid “zero data retention” as a blanket claim; avoid implying we store/no-store...

Pilot guardrails (please read)

- Compliance accuracy is non-negotiable: no “HIPAA-certified/HIPAA-ready” language; avoid implying certifications we don’t have. Use approved claims only.
- Evidence pack sharing must follow confidentiality guidance; SOC 2 report only via approved process.

text:
Purpose
- Working target account list for the 6–8 week ABM pilot focused on regulated workloads (FinServ + Healthcare).
- This sheet is used for: territory assignment, outreach sequencing, and weekly pipeline/coverage review.

Scoring rubric (prioritization)
- Total Score = Fit (0–5) + Intent (0–5) + Access (0–5) + Timing (0–5) + Deployment Need (0–5). Max = 25.

Definitions
- Fit: Regulated workload likelihood + scale (transaction volume, call center load, internal ops) + engineering maturity.
- Intent: Evidence of active search (website visits, benchmark downloads, webinar reg, partner co-sell signal, inbound security questions).
- Access: Known champions / reachable personas (Platform/ML Eng, Security/GRC, Proc/Legal) + warm intros.
- Timing: Stated/observed timeline for evaluation/purchase in 0–6 months.
- Deployment Need: Strong signal for Dedicated/Private (VPC, on-prem, KMS/HSM...
